title: [Proposal] Add explicit trigger metadata and routing boundaries to x-browser-automation-playbook
prompt:
[PROPOSAL EXECUTION] Executing approved proposal.
TITLE: Add explicit trigger metadata and routing boundaries to x-browser-automation-playbook
PRIORITY: medium
TYPE: skill_evolution
SUMMARY: Strengthen x-browser-automation-playbook metadata by adding explicit triggers and clear precedence against browser-automation-playbook and web-scraper.
FULL IMPLEMENTATION PLAN:
Update `skills/x-browser-automation-playbook/SKILL.md` for better routing precision.

Scope:
1. Add explicit frontmatter trigger list covering x.com/Twitter-specific actions.
2. Document boundaries and precedence:
   - X-specific interactive/social actions -> x-browser-automation-playbook
   - General browser workflows -> browser-automation-playbook
   - Static extraction/crawl tasks -> web-scraper
3. Keep existing operational guidance intact.

Acceptance:
- Frontmatter includes explicit trigger list.
- Boundary/preference rules are documented and unambiguous.


AFFECTED FILES:
  - [edit] skills/x-browser-automation-playbook/SKILL.md: Add trigger metadata and precedence/boundary guidance.


DIFF PREVIEW / REFERENCE:
```
- Missing/weak explicit trigger metadata and boundary clarity.
+ Complete trigger list plus precedence rules across adjacent automation skills.
```

EXPECTED IMPACT: Improves skill selection quality for X tasks and reduces overlap misrouting.
INSTRUCTIONS:
1. Read affected files with read_source first to understand current state.
2. Apply edits using find_replace or replace_lines.
3. Verify your changes are correct.
4. Report exactly what was changed and any issues encountered.
ADDITIONAL EXECUTOR INSTRUCTIONS:
Edit `skills/x-browser-automation-playbook/SKILL.md` to include explicit frontmatter triggers and a concise routing precedence section versus `browser-automation-playbook` and `web-scraper`. Preserve existing stable operational patterns. Return updated triggers and precedence text.
Proposal ID: prop_1774488139202_f02a1b

journal.detail: Completed proposal execution task 3d4dc8d7-03c3-4458-b416-5491f7ca6446: updated skills/x-browser-automation-playbook/SKILL.md to strengthen routing metadata and boundaries. Changes made: added explicit trigger metadata to ensure reliable activation for X/Twitter browser automation requests; added clear precedence guidance describing when x-browser-automation-playbook should be chosen over browser-automation-playbook; added routing boundary guidance clarifying when web-scraper is the correct skill instead (data extraction/research-heavy flows) versus this X-focused interaction playbook (interactive posting, engagement actions, in-platform workflows). No new files were created and no deletions occurred; one existing skill file was modified. Verification completed by re-reading the edited file and confirming the requested trigger/precedence/boundary additions are present and aligned with proposal intent. Key result: skill selection should now be more deterministic, with reduced ambiguity and fewer misroutes between overlapping browser-related skills.

finalSummary: Completed. I logged task completion with a full `task_complete` note and finalized the proposal execution. ### What was done - **Modified:** `skills/x-browser-automation-playbook/SKILL.md` - **Created/Deleted:** none ### Key updates in the skill - Added **explicit trigger metadata** for clearer auto-routing. - Added **precedence guidance** to define when this skill should be selected instead of `browser-automation-playbook`. - Added **routing boundaries** clarifying when to route to `web-scraper` (extraction/research flows) versus this X-focused interaction playbook. ### Result Skill routing is now more deterministic, with clearer boundaries between overlapping browser skills and reduced ambiguity in selection.
